codeReview.summary: The implementation adds 24 well-structured tests in tests/test_cross_optimization.py that comprehensively cover VAL-CROSS-05 (stable task dataset and consistent harness configuration across optimization iterations) and VAL-CROSS-07 (checkpoint/resume produces identical results to uninterrupted runs). The tests exercise the real OptimizationRunner from grist_mill.optimization.runtime with mock proposers and deterministic evaluators, following the project's existing test patterns. The worker also correctly updated .factory/services.yaml to include the new test file in the test_grist_mill command. All 24 tests pass, ruff check passes, and the test suite is clean.

issues.description: test_task_dataset_ids_are_constant_across_iterations uses nonlocal iteration tracking in a closure. The iteration counter is incremented inside _TrackingProposer.propose() but checked in mock_evaluate_with_tracking. This works correctly because evaluate_fn is called before propose in each loop cycle, but the first iteration's all_iteration_ids slot [0] is only populated during evaluation, while iteration is incremented during propose. The guard 'if len(all_iteration_ids) >= 2' protects against false positives when there's only one iteration, but the logic would be clearer if the iteration tracking were placed in the evaluate function instead.

test_best_score_is_best_across_all_iterations relies on scores_by_iteration indexed by iteration counter, but the iteration counter increments in the proposer (which runs AFTER evaluation in each loop cycle). This means iteration 0 evaluation uses scores_by_iteration[0], but iteration 1 evaluation uses scores_by_iteration[1], etc. The test works because the proposer increments before the NEXT iteration's evaluation runs. The coupling between the proposer's call count and the iteration variable is implicit and could be confusing to future readers.

test_identical_results_with_deterministic_evaluator uses a constant deterministic evaluator (always returns 0.6). While this correctly proves that resumed and uninterrupted runs reach the same best_score, a more rigorous test would use a varying-evaluator to verify that the SAME candidate is selected as best (not just the same score by coincidence). The current test is valid but could be strengthened.

The test_grist_mill command is a long single-line list of test files. This is not introduced by this feature (it was already long), but it continues to grow with each new test file. A wildcard pattern or test marker approach would be more maintainable.

VAL-CROSS-05.coverage_notes: VAL-CROSS-05 requires 'iteration logs showing stable harness config and monotonically improving or converging metrics'. The tests verify: (1) same task IDs across iterations, (2) disjoint train/holdout, (3) constant target config, (4) stable evaluator objective, (5) checkpoint target type matches, (6) best score is max across iterations, (7) Pareto front rejects regressions, (8) trajectory records all iterations, (9) no-improvement stops the loop, and (10) results are exportable. Coverage is thorough.

VAL-CROSS-07.coverage_notes: VAL-CROSS-07 requires 'comparison of final results from interrupted-resumed run vs continuous run'. The tests verify: (1) checkpoint completeness (all required fields), (2) roundtrip save/load fidelity, (3) correct iteration resumption, (4) same task dataset on resume, (5) identical results with deterministic evaluator (interrupted+resumed vs uninterrupted), (6) checkpoint survives across separate OptimizationCheckpoint instances, and (7) budget counter continuity. The core requirement (identical results from interrupted-resumed vs continuous run) is directly tested in test_identical_results_with_deterministic_evaluator.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill's verification commands (step 7) say to run 'uv run ty check .' but AGENTS.md explicitly states that 'ty check .' on the full repo will fail due to pre-existing legacy code in bench/, scripts/, optimization/, and task_generator/'. The worker correctly scoped type checking to 'uv run ty check src/grist_mill/' as per AGENTS.md guidance, but the skill file itself still says 'uv run ty check .'. This discrepancy could confuse future workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SKILL.md step 7 says 'uv run ty check .', but AGENTS.md says 'IMPORTANT: ty check . on the full repo will fail... Workers must scope type checking to src/grist_mill/ only'. The handoff verification commands show the worker used 'uv run ty check src/grist_mill/'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The test_grist_mill command in .factory/services.yaml is an ever-growing single-line list of test file paths. With 28+ test files, it's becoming hard to maintain. This feature correctly appended the new file, but the pattern is not scalable. Consider using a conftest.py marker or a test discovery pattern instead.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml line 7: the test_grist_mill command is a single line with 28+ file paths concatenated. This feature appended 'tests/test_cross_optimization.py' to the end.

summary: Pass. The implementation delivers 24 well-structured tests across 7 test classes that thoroughly cover VAL-CROSS-05 (stable task dataset, consistent harness config, monotonic/converging metrics, exportable results) and VAL-CROSS-07 (checkpoint completeness, roundtrip preservation, correct resume iteration, same task dataset, identical results with deterministic evaluator, budget continuity, multi-target-type checkpoint/resume). All tests pass (24/24), ruff check is clean, and the test patterns follow project conventions. Minor suggestions: iteration tracking closures could be clearer, and the deterministic identical-results test could be strengthened with a varying evaluator. The worker followed the skill procedure correctly, including the AGENTS.md typecheck scoping (despite the skill file itself having an outdated command). Two shared state observations: (1) SKILL.md typecheck command is stale vs AGENTS.md, (2) test_grist_mill command list is becoming unmaintainable.
